summary: Kühn, Osthus, and Townsend (2016) proved that $f_t(k,\ldots,k) = O(k^7 t^4)$ exists, giving an affirmative answer to Thomassen's partition question for the uniform case; the general non-uniform case (different $k_i$) follows trivially by setting $k = \max_i k_i$. Girão and Letzter (2025) subsequently confirmed the optimal linear bound $f_t(k,\ldots,k) = O(kt)$, matching the lower bound up to a constant.

since_posted.title: Proof of a tournament partition conjecture and an application to 1-factors with prescribed cycle lengths
since_posted.authors: Daniela Kühn, Deryk Osthus, Timothy Townsend
since_posted.claim: Proves existence of $f_t(k,\ldots,k) = O(k^7 t^4)$, establishing an affirmative answer to Thomassen's question: every sufficiently strongly connected tournament can be partitioned into $t$ parts each inducing a strongly $k$-connected subtournament.

since_posted.title: Partitioning a tournament into sub-tournaments of high connectivity
since_posted.claim: Proves the optimal linear bound $f_t(k,\ldots,k) = O(kt)$, confirming a 2016 conjecture of Kühn, Osthus, and Townsend and showing the bound is tight up to a constant factor.

search_queries: partition tournament strongly connected subtournaments Thomassen conjecture
g(k1,...,kp) strong tournament partition k-strongly connected subtournaments existence
partition tournament two strongly connected subtournaments g(2,2) open problem
arXiv tournament partition strongly connected components subtournament 2014 2015 2016 2017 2018 2019 2020 2021 2022 2023 2024
notes: The Kühn-Osthus-Townsend paper (arXiv:1309.7677, submitted 2013-09-29, published Combinatorica ~2016) formally treats the uniform case (all $k_i = k$). The non-uniform Thomassen problem reduces to the uniform case via $k = \max_i k_i$, so $g(k_1,\ldots,k_p) \leq f_p(\max_i k_i, \ldots, \max_i k_i)$. The Springer pages for both Combinatorica articles were authentication-blocked; publication years and DOIs are inferred from arXiv metadata (last revision June 2025 for Girão-Letzter) and DOI string patterns. The Girão-Letzter paper (arXiv:2210.17371) was confirmed published in Combinatorica with DOI 10.1007/s00493-025-00161-3.
